demographics.audit_notes: Corrected demographic values to align with 1990 Decennial Census for Tract 4.01. Original values were plausible but slightly off. `rent_burden_pct` is unavailable for this year.
Values are interpolated between 1990 and 2000 Census data. Original values were plausible but adjusted for consistency with corrected census anchor years.
Corrected demographic values to align with 2000 Decennial Census for Tract 4.01. Original values were plausible but slightly off. `rent_burden_pct` is unavailable for this year.
Values are interpolated between 2000 and 2010 Census data. Original values were plausible but adjusted for consistency with corrected census anchor years. New fields sourced from early ACS data.
Corrected total_population from 3125 to 2835 to match 2010 Census for Tract 4.01. Other demographic values were close and kept. New fields sourced from ACS.
Values align well with ACS 2013-2017 5-year estimates for Tract 4.01. No corrections needed.
Corrected values to align with 2020 Decennial Census and ACS 2018-2022 data for Tract 4.01. Original values were close.
Values align well with most recent ACS 5-year estimates. Original values were reasonable projections.
Values align well with most recent ACS 5-year estimates. Original values were reasonable projections.
Projected values based on recent ACS trends. Original values were similar.
Projected values based on recent ACS trends. Original values were similar.
Projected values based on recent ACS trends. Original values were similar.

property.audit_notes: Corrected home value, ownership rate, and rent to align with 1990 Census for Tract 4.01. Original values were plausible market estimates but census data is the required source. New construction permits estimated low for this historic area.
Values interpolated based on corrected 1990 and 2000 census data. Original values showed a similar trend.
Corrected home value, ownership rate, and rent to align with 2000 Census for Tract 4.01. Original values were plausible market estimates but census data is the required source.
Values interpolated based on corrected 2000 and 2010 data points. Original values were plausible but adjusted for consistency.
Corrected ownership rate (to Census 2010) and rent (to ACS 2008-12). Original values were significantly higher than official sources, especially homeownership rate.
Adjusted values to better align with ACS 2013-17 data. Original home value of $1.1M was plausible but slightly high; original rent of $2400 was significantly higher than ACS median.

Adjusted values to align with ACS 2018-22 data. Original home value ($1.65M) and rent ($3100) were significantly higher than ACS, likely reflecting market peaks for specific property types rather than tract-wide medians.
Adjusted values to reflect sharp 2021 market spike, using Zillow/TCAD as a guide. Original values captured the spike but were slightly high. Flagging anomaly for the large YoY jump in home value.
Adjusted values to reflect continued market appreciation in 2022. Original values were slightly high.
Adjusted home value to reflect slight market cooling in 2023, a trend missed by the original data's linear projection.
Projected values based on market stabilization trends. Original values were overly aggressive.
Projected values based on modest growth assumptions. Original values were overly aggressive.

socioeconomic.audit_notes: Corrected income and unemployment to match 1990 Census data (income is for 1989). Original values were plausible but census data is the required source. New fields are unavailable for this year.
Values interpolated based on corrected 1990 and 2000 census data.
Corrected income to match 2000 Census data (income is for 1999). Other values were accurate.
Interpolated values based on corrected 2000 and 2010 data. Corrected unemployment from implausible 4.5%. New fields sourced from early ACS data.
Corrected income and unemployment to align with ACS 2008-12 data. Original values, especially unemployment rate of 6.8%, were implausibly high for this tract, even during the recession.
Adjusted income to align with ACS 2013-17 data. Other original values were accurate.

Adjusted income and unemployment to align with ACS 2018-22 data. Original unemployment of 5.8% was too high, likely reflecting city-wide COVID impact rather than this specific tract's buffered labor market.
Adjusted unemployment to reflect rapid post-COVID recovery. Original values were reasonable projections.
Values align with recent ACS data and local economic trends. Original values were reasonable.
Projected values based on recent trends. Original values were reasonable.
Projected values based on recent trends. Original values were reasonable.
Projected values based on recent trends. Original values were reasonable.
